metadata.title: Hub for European Youth · Acerno
metadata.description: Authentic hospitality in a 17th-century Franciscan convent in the heart of the Picentini Mountains. Dorms and private rooms around the cloister, in Acerno (SA), Italy.

convento.title: The Convent of Sant'Antonio · Hub for European Youth
convento.description: Four centuries of history: the arcaded cloister, the resurfaced frescoes and the spaces of the Franciscan complex in Acerno, today a place of hospitality.

camere.title: Rooms and dorms · Hub for European Youth
camere.description: Group dorms and private rooms around the cloister: vaulted ceilings, terracotta, calm light. Availability and rates on request.

contatti.title: Contact and directions · Hub for European Youth
contatti.description: We are in the centre of Acerno, in the Picentini Mountains, province of Salerno. How to reach us and how to write to us.

prenota.title: Request a booking · Hub for European Youth
prenota.description: Pick your dates and room: we save your request and get back to you to confirm. No online payment.

hero.title: A 17th-century convent,
hero.titleAccent: a home for those who walk
hero.subtitle: Simple, warm hospitality around a stone cloister. Dorms for groups, private rooms for families and travellers, a step away from the trails of the Park.
hero.imageAlt: Arcaded convent cloister with arches on stone columns

intro.title: A place that has welcomed for four centuries
intro.body1: The Hub for European Youth is located inside the suggestive 17th-century Franciscan complex in the centre of Acerno, restored and given back to the community as a place of sustainable, accessible hospitality.
intro.body2: It is run by Moby Dick ETS, a non-profit social enterprise: no showy luxury, but authenticity, care for the place and attention to everyone — groups, school trips, hikers, pilgrims and families.

chiostro.title: The cloister
chiostro.body: The heart of the building: a portico of arches on stone columns, with a garden and a well. Monastic quiet in the middle of the village.

accessibilita.title: Accessible to all
accessibilita.body: Thoughtful accessibility and honest prices: here hospitality is a right, not a privilege.

territorio.title: In the Picentini Mountains
territorio.body: Hiking, nature, horse riding and good food: Acerno is the ideal base to explore the Regional Park.

rotta.title: Every route begins at a starting point.
rotta.body: The Hub for European Youth is where ideas become directions and aspirations start to take shape.

orizzonti.title: Every encounter opens new horizons.
orizzonti.body: The cultures, languages and experiences we meet along the way transform how we see the world and ourselves.

persone.title: The most important routes don't cross the sea. They cross people.
persone.body: The Hub is a safe harbour to set out from, explore, and return to with new skills, relationships and opportunities.

rooms.title: Dorms and rooms around the cloister
rooms.body: Around twenty rooms on the first floor, with vaulted or beamed ceilings and terracotta floors. From group dorms to private rooms.

cta.title: Want to come and stay?
cta.body: Pick your dates and tell us about you: we'll reply with availability and details. No online payment, just a request.

hero.title: The Convent of Sant'Antonio
hero.subtitle: A monastic courtyard complex, dedicated to Saint Anthony, in the centre of Acerno.
hero.imageAlt: View of the cloister and the vaulted spaces of the convent

story.title: Four centuries, one calling: to welcome
story.body1: A 17th-century Franciscan convent, arranged around a central arcaded cloister — arches on stone columns, a garden with a well — and connected to a single-nave church.
story.body2: Damaged by the 1980 earthquake, it was restored and turned into a youth hostel. During the works, 17th- and 18th-century frescoes resurfaced, today part of the charm of the place.
story.body3: The church is not part of the hostel: hospitality lives in the convent's spaces, among terracotta, vaults and silence.

fondazione.title: The foundation
fondazione.body: In the centre of Acerno the Franciscan complex dedicated to Saint Anthony is born: a courtyard monastic layout, the arcaded cloister with its well, the single-nave church.

vita.title: Life in the convent
vita.body: Centuries of monastic life around the cloister: vaults are frescoed, spaces layer up between prayer, work and the welcome of travellers.

terremoto.title: The earthquake
terremoto.body: The Irpinia earthquake wounds the complex. The convent empties, but the village does not forget it.

recupero.title: The recovery
recupero.body: Restoration gives the spaces back and brings the 17th–18th-century frescoes to light. The convent is reborn as a youth hostel.

oggi.title: The Hub
oggi.body: With Moby Dick ETS the complex becomes the Hub for European Youth: sustainable, social, accessible hospitality — once again a home for those who walk.

spaces.title: The spaces
spaces.intro: Three levels that tell the life of the convent.

pianoTerra.title: Ground floor
pianoTerra.body: Reception in the entrance hall, an office, a meeting room and a dining hall for about 50 with meal service. The cloister is the visual heart; the sacristy remains.

primoPiano.title: First floor
primoPiano.body: Around twenty rooms around the cloister: a mix of 3–4 single-bed dorms and private rooms, vaulted or beamed ceilings, terracotta floors and shared bathrooms. Accessible options available.

seminterrato.title: Lower level
seminterrato.body: Laundry and ironing room, pantry, boiler room and storage, together with historic spaces — the cistern and the hypogeum — to be valued as a point of interest.

plan.title: The courtyard layout
plan.body: The architectural plans show the monastic layout: the rooms are arranged around the cloister, following the logic of the courtyard.
plan.alt: Architectural plan of the convent with the central cloister

gallery.affreschiAlt: 17th–18th-century frescoes resurfaced in the convent church
gallery.affreschiCaption: The frescoes that resurfaced during restoration
gallery.volteAlt: Cross vaults of the portico seen from below
gallery.volteCaption: The cross vaults of the portico

hero.title: The rooms
hero.subtitle: Group dorms and private rooms, all facing or near the cloister. Vaulted ceilings, terracotta, calm light.

intro.body: Availability and rates are confirmed on request: pick a room and send us a request, with no online payment.

empty.title: Rooms coming soon
empty.body: We're finishing the accommodation catalogue. Write to us in the meantime — we'll be glad to find you a place.

hero.title: Come and visit
hero.subtitle: We're in the centre of Acerno, in the Picentini Mountains in the province of Salerno.

org.title: The organisation
org.body: The venue is run by Moby Dick ETS, a non-profit third-sector organisation based in Salerno.

how.title: Getting here
how.body: Acerno is reachable by car from the A2 / Salerno–Avellino road. About an hour from the Salerno coast through the woods of the Park. Write to us for directions tailored to your group.

map.label: Map
map.iframeTitle: Google map of the Convent of Sant'Antonio in Acerno
map.photoAlt: The convent façade with its entrance portal, in the centre of Acerno

hero.title: Request a booking
hero.subtitle: Pick your dates and room: we save your request and get back to you to confirm. No online payment.

form.messagePlaceholder: We're a group, travelling with a dog, arriving late in the evening…

errors.name: Please enter your name (min. 2 characters).
errors.checkInFuture: Check-in cannot be in the past.
errors.guestsMax: Too many guests for this accommodation.

availability.count: {count, plural, one {# accommodation available} other {# accommodations available}} for your dates
availability.none: No accommodation available for these dates. Try different dates or write to us.

success.title: Request received, thank you!
success.body: We've saved your request for {room} from {checkIn} to {checkOut}. We'll get back to you soon by email to confirm availability and details.

notFound.title: Off the charts
notFound.body: The page you were looking for isn't on this map. Head back to harbour and set out again.

territorio.title: The Picentini Mountains around us
territorio.body: Acerno sits inside the Picentini Mountains Regional Park: trails, woods, waterfalls, horse riding and local food within a backpack's reach. The chart marks the routes to set out on.
territorio.mapLabel: Illustrated chart of the surroundings of Acerno

orari.q: What are the check-in and check-out times?
orari.a: Check-in is from 3pm to 8pm, check-out by 10am. For arrivals outside these hours, tell us in your request message and we'll arrange it.

gruppi.q: Do you host groups and school trips?
gruppi.a: Yes, they're most welcome: our 3-4 bed dorms are made for it and the dining hall seats about 50. For groups it's best to write a little in advance so we can tailor everything.

accessibilita.a: Accessibility is part of our mission: some accommodations are designed for reduced mobility. Tell us your needs in the request and we'll confirm the best solution.

animali.a: Well-behaved animals are welcome in some accommodations: mention it in your request and we'll assign you the right room.

pasti.q: Can I eat at the hostel?
pasti.a: The dining hall (~50 seats) serves meals on reservation, ideal for groups and school trips. The village also offers trattorias and Picentini food and wine.

tariffe.q: How much does a night cost?
tariffe.a: We are a non-profit third-sector organisation: rates are fair and shared on request, based on season, group and accommodation. Send a request or write to us and we'll reply with a quote.

steps.monti: Acerno, high in the Picentini Mountains
steps.chiostro: A 17th-century cloister, four hundred years of quiet
steps.porta: The door is open. Welcome to the Hub for European Youth.

login.title: Restricted area
login.subtitle: Sign in to manage booking requests.

dashboard.title: Booking requests
dashboard.subtitle: Confirm or decline requests: the guest receives an email with the outcome.

about.title: The Hub
about.body: Sustainable, accessible hospitality in a 17th-century Franciscan convent, in the centre of Acerno.

footer.org: Run by Moby Dick ETS — non-profit social enterprise
footer.mission: Sustainable, social and accessible tourism for the Salerno region.
